text: The burden of the word of the Lord to Israel by the hand of Malachias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have loved you, saith the Lord: and you have said: Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau brother to Jacob, saith the Lord, and I have loved Jacob,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But have hated Esau? and I have made his mountains a wilderness, and given his inheritance to the dragons of the desert.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if Edom shall say: We are destroyed, but we will return and build up what hath been destroyed: thus saith the Lord of hosts: They shall build up, and I will throw down: and they shall be called the borders of wickedness, and the people with whom the Lord is angry for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your eyes shall see: and you shall say: The Lord be magnified upon the border of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The son honoureth the father, and the servant his master: if then I be a father, where is my honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear? saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To you, O priests, that despise my name, and have said: Wherein have we despised thy name? You offer polluted bread upon my altar, and you say: Wherein have we polluted thee? In that you say: The table of the Lord is contemptible.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if you offer the lame and the sick, is it not evil? offer it to thy prince, if he will be pleased with it, or if he will regard thy face, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now beseech ye the face of God, that he may have mercy on you, (for by your hand hath this been done,) if by any means he will receive your faces, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is there among you, that will shut the doors, and will kindle the fire on my altar gratis? I have no pleasure in you, saith the Lord of hosts: and I will not receive a gift of your hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For from the rising of the sun even to the going down, my name is great among the Gentiles, and in every place there is sacrifice, and there is offered to my name a clean oblation: for my name is great among the Gentiles, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you have profaned it in that you say: The table of the Lord is defiled: and that which is laid thereupon is contemptible with the fire that devoureth it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you have said: Behold of our labour, and you puffed it away, saith the Lord of hosts, and you brought in of rapine the lame, and the sick, and brought in an offering: shall I accept it at your hands, saith the Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed is the deceitful man that hath in his flock a male, and making a vow offereth in sacrifice that which is feeble to the Lord: for I am a great King, saith the Lord of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the Gentiles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O ye priests, this commandment is to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you will not hear, and if you will not lay it to heart, to give glory to my name, saith the Lord of hosts: I will send poverty upon you, and will curse your blessings, yea I will curse them, because you have not laid it to heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I will cast the shoulder to you, and will scatter upon your face the dung of your solemnities, and it shall take you away with it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall know that I sent you this commandment, that my covenant might be with Levi, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My covenant was with him of life and peace: and I gave him fear: and he feared me, and he was afraid before my name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The law of truth was in his mouth, and iniquity was not found in his lips: he walked with me in peace, and in equity, and turned many away from iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the lips of the priest shall keep knowledge, and they shall seek the law at his mouth: because he is the angel of the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you have departed out of the way, and have caused many to stumble at the law: you have made void the covenant of Levi, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore have I also made you contemptible, and base before all people, as you have not kept my ways, and have accepted persons in the law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Have we not all one father? hath not one God created us? why then doth every one of us despise his brother, violating the covenant of our fathers?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Juda hath transgressed, and abomination hath been committed in Israel, and in Jerusalem: for Juda hath profaned the holiness of the Lord, which he loved, and hath married the daughter of a strange god.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will cut off the man that hath done this, both the master, and the scholar, out of the tabernacles of Jacob, and him that offereth an offering to the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this again have you done, you have covered the altar of the Lord with tears, with weeping, and bellowing, so that I have no more a regard to sacrifice, neither do I accept any atonement at your hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you have said: For what cause? Because the Lord hath been witness between thee, and the wife of thy youth, whom thou hast despised: yet she was thy partner, and the wife of thy covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did not one make her, and she is the residue of his spirit? And what doth one seek, but the seed of God? Keep then your spirit, and despise not the wife of thy youth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt hate her put her away, saith the Lord, the God of Israel: but iniquity shall cover his garment, saith the Lord of hosts, keep your spirit, and despise not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have wearied the Lord with your words, and you said: Wherein have we wearied him? In that you say: Every one that doth evil, is good in the sight of the Lord, and such please him: or surely where is the God of judgment?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I send my angel, and he shall prepare the way before my face. And presently the Lord, whom you seek, and the angel of the testament, whom you desire, shall come to his temple. Behold, he cometh, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And who shall be able to think of the day of his coming? and who shall stand to see him? for he is like a refining fire, and like the fuller’s herb:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall sit refining and cleansing the silver, and he shall purify the sons of Levi, and shall refine them as gold, and as silver, and they shall offer sacrifices to the Lord in justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sacrifice of Juda and of Jerusalem shall please the Lord, as in the days of old, and in the ancient years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will come to you in judgment, and will be a speedy witness against sorcerers, and adulterers, and false swearers, and them that oppress the hireling in his wages, the widows, and the fatherless: and oppress the stranger, and have not feared me, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am the Lord, and I change not: and you the sons of Jacob are not consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For from the days of your fathers you have departed from my ordinances, and have not kept them: Return to me, and I will return to you, saith the Lord of hosts. And you have said: Wherein shall we return?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall a man afflict God? for you afflict me. And you have said: Wherein do we afflict thee? in tithes and in firstfruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you are cursed with want, and you afflict me, even the whole nation of you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in my house, and try me in this, saith the Lord: if I open not unto you the flood-gates of heaven, and pour you out a blessing even to abundance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will rebuke for your sakes the devourer, and he shall not spoil the fruit of your land: neither shall the vine in the field be barren, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all nations shall call you blessed: for you shall be a delightful land, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your words have been unsufferable to me, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you have said: What have we spoken against thee? You have said: He laboureth in vain that serveth God, and what profit is it that we have kept his ordinances, and that we have walked sorrowful before the Lord of hosts?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore now we call the proud people happy, for they that work wickedness are built up, and they have tempted God and are preserved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then they that feared the Lord, spoke every one with his neighbour: and the Lord gave ear, and heard it: and a book of remembrance was written before him for them that fear the Lord, and think on his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be my special possession, saith the Lord of hosts, in the day that I do judgment: and I will spare them, as a man spareth his son that serveth him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall return, and shall see the difference between the just and the wicked: and between him that serveth God, and him that serveth him not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold the day shall come kindled as a furnace: and all the proud, and all that do wickedly shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall set them on fire, saith the Lord of hosts, it shall not leave them root, nor branch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But unto you that fear my name, the Sun of justice shall arise, and health in his wings: and you shall go forth, and shall leap like calves of the herd.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall tread down the wicked when they shall be ashes under the sole of your feet in the day that I do this, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember the law of Moses my servant, which I commanded him in Horeb for all Israel, the precepts, and judgments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I will send you Elias the prophet, before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall turn the heart of the fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their fathers: lest I come, and strike the earth with anathema.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
